examples.title: Basic Function Overloading
examples.description: Demonstrates function overloading with different number of parameters
examples.explanation: Three overloaded add() functions exist: two integers (2 params), three integers, and two doubles. Compiler selects based on argument types and count.

examples.title: Constructor Overloading
examples.description: Shows multiple constructor definitions in a class
examples.explanation: Three different constructors create objects in different ways: default initializes to 1s, parameterized takes dimensions, copy creates duplicate.

examples.title: Ambiguity with Type Conversion
examples.description: Demonstrates ambiguity that arises when multiple conversions are possible
examples.explanation: print('A') causes ambiguity because character 'A' can convert to both int (ASCII 65) and double equally. The compiler cannot decide which function to call.

examples.title: Const Parameter Overloading
examples.description: Shows how const and non-const parameters create different overloads
examples.explanation: getChar() has two versions: one for modifying characters (non-const) and one for read-only access (const). Compiler selects based on object const-ness.

examples.title: Function Overloading with Different Parameter Order
examples.description: Demonstrates overloading with reversed parameter types
examples.explanation: Both overloads have same types but different order. The compiler matches based on exact argument types and their positions in the parameter list.
